title: Dasiphora fruticosa tenuifolia
intro: Dasiphora fruticosa tenuifolia, commonly known as a type of shrub or dwarf shrub, is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dasiphora fruticosa tenuifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various medicinal formulations such as capsules and extracts.
taxonomy: Dasiphora fruticosa tenuifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the rose family. This plant is also known as the Russian sage or burnet rose. It belongs to the genus Dasiphora, which is a group of flowering plants native to Asia. The species name tenuifolia refers to the plant's narrow leaves. Dasiphora fruticosa tenuifolia is a shrubby perennial plant that is widely cultivated for its ornamental value.
